title: Rich Roasted Tunisian Oxtail Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Tunisian dish featuring oxtail, perfect for a sick day, with a crispy exterior and tender interior, served in a satvik-friendly format, the oxtail is shaped like nigiri for a unique presentation.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the oxtail pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add 1 cup of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the oxtail for 2 1/2 hours, or until it is tender and falling off the bone.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Shape the oxtail into nigiri shapes and serve.
